revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: ''''土坑墓'''（どこうぼ）とは、土を掘りくぼめて穴（[[土坑]]）をつくり、そこに人の遺体を納めて[[葬送]]した[[遺構]]。...'
revision.text:
'''土坑墓'''（どこうぼ）とは、土を掘りくぼめて穴（[[土坑]]）をつくり、そこに人の遺体を納めて[[葬送]]した[[遺構]]。[[縄文時代]]～[[弥生時代]]に多い墓の形式である。
==縄文時代の土坑墓の諸特徴== #径はおおむね0.7～1.5mの範囲。平面形状は円形または方形で、多くは円形を呈する #深さは平面規模に比べて浅く、底が平坦である #覆土はたいていの場合、一括埋め戻しの人為堆積である #[[人骨]]をともなう場合もあるが、まったく痕跡をとどめない場合も多い #[[副葬品]]をともなう場合がある。後期・晩期になると質・量ともに充実し、特に晩期には工芸品的な価値の高いものが副葬される場合が多くなる
縄文時代の葬送は[[貝塚]]などから見つかる[[人骨]]のようすから、[[屈葬]]が一般的だったと考えられる。規模や形状はそれを反映したものととらえることができる。[[礫]]を土坑の中や外に配置した墓は特に'''配石墓'''と呼ばれる。
副葬品や人骨が出土しなくても、覆土の土壌分析とくに[[脂肪酸]]分析によって土坑墓と判別される場合がある。
==関連項目== *[[土坑]] *[[屈葬]]
{{stub}}
[[Category:考古学|とこうほ]] [[Category:縄文時代|とこうほ]] [[Category:弥生時代|とこうほ]]

'''土坑墓'''（どこうぼ）とは、土を掘りくぼめて穴（[[土坑]]）をつくり、そこに人の遺体を納めて[[葬送]]した[[遺構]]。世界的にポピュラーな埋葬に用いられた遺構であるが、日本では[[縄文時代]]～[[弥生時代]]に多い墓の形式であって、その場合「土壙」とか「土壙墓」と表記される場合もある。
==縄文時代の土坑墓の諸特徴== #径はおおむね0.7～1.5mの範囲。平面形状は円形または方形で、多くは円形を呈する #深さは平面規模に比べて浅く、底が平坦である #覆土はたいていの場合、一括埋め戻しの人為堆積である #[[人骨]]をともなう場合もあるが、まったく痕跡をとどめない場合も多い #[[副葬品]]をともなう場合がある。後期・晩期になると質・量ともに充実し、特に晩期には工芸品的な価値の高いものが副葬される場合が多くなる
縄文時代の葬送は[[貝塚]]などから見つかる[[人骨]]のようすから、[[屈葬]]が一般的だったと考えられる。規模や形状はそれを反映したものととらえることができる。[[礫]]を土坑の中や外に配置した墓は特に'''配石墓'''と呼ばれる。
副葬品や人骨が出土しなくても、覆土の土壌分析とくに[[脂肪酸]]分析によって土坑墓と判別される場合がある。
==関連項目== *[[土坑]] *[[屈葬]] *[[木棺墓]] *[[甕棺墓]] *[[石棺墓]]
{{stub}}
[[Category:考古学|とこうほ]] [[Category:縄文時代|とこうほ]] [[Category:弥生時代|とこうほ]]

縄文時代の葬送は[[貝塚]]などから見つかる人骨のようすから、[[屈葬]]が一般的だったと考えられる。規模や形状はそれを反映したものととらえることができる。[[礫]]を土坑の中や外に配置した墓は特に'''配石墓'''と呼ばれる。
副葬品や人骨が出土しなくても、覆土の土壌分析とくに[[脂肪酸]]分析によって土坑墓と判別される場合がある。

'''土坑墓'''（どこうぼ）とは、土を掘りくぼめて穴（[[土坑]]）をつくり、そこに人の遺体を納めて[[葬送]]した[[遺構]]。世界的にポピュラーな埋葬に用いられた遺構であるが、日本では[[縄文時代]]～[[弥生時代]]に多い墓の形式であって、その場合「土壙」とか「土壙墓」と表記される場合もある。
==旧石器時代の土坑墓== [[大阪府]][[藤井寺市]]の[[はさみ山遺跡]]梨田地点から、墓と推定される楕円形の土坑が確認されている。
==縄文時代の土坑墓の諸特徴== #径はおおむね0.7～1.5mの範囲。平面形状は円形または方形で、多くは円形を呈する #深さは平面規模に比べて浅く、底が平坦である #[[覆土]]はたいていの場合、一括埋め戻しの人為堆積である #[[人骨]]をともなう場合もあるが、まったく痕跡をとどめない場合も多い #[[副葬品]]をともなう場合がある。後期・晩期になると質・量ともに充実し、特に晩期には工芸品的な価値の高いものが副葬される場合が多くなる
縄文時代の葬送は[[貝塚]]などから見つかる人骨のようすから、[[屈葬]]が一般的だったと考えられる。規模や形状はそれを反映したものととらえることができる。[[礫]]を土坑の中や外に配置した墓は特に'''配石墓'''と呼ばれる。
副葬品や人骨が出土しなくても、覆土の土壌分析とくに[[脂肪酸]]分析によって土坑墓と判別される場合がある。

revision.comment: 新節を新設、加筆
revision.text:
'''土坑墓'''（どこうぼ）とは、土を掘りくぼめて穴（[[土坑]]）をつくり、そこに人の遺体を納めて[[葬送]]した[[遺構]]。世界的にポピュラーな埋葬に用いられた遺構であるが、日本では[[縄文時代]]～[[弥生時代]]に多い墓の形式であって、その場合「土壙」とか「土壙墓」と表記される場合もある。
==旧石器時代の土坑墓== [[大阪府]][[藤井寺市]]の[[はさみ山遺跡]]梨田地点から、墓と推定される楕円形の土坑が確認されている。
==縄文時代の土坑墓の諸特徴== #径はおおむね0.7～1.5mの範囲。平面形状は円形または方形で、多くは円形を呈する #深さは平面規模に比べて浅く、底が平坦である #[[覆土]]はたいていの場合、一括埋め戻しの人為堆積である #[[人骨]]をともなう場合もあるが、まったく痕跡をとどめない場合も多い #[[副葬品]]をともなう場合がある。後期・晩期になると質・量ともに充実し、特に晩期には工芸品的な価値の高いものが副葬される場合が多くなる
縄文時代の葬送は[[貝塚]]などから見つかる人骨のようすから、[[屈葬]]が一般的だったと考えられる。規模や形状はそれを反映したものととらえることができる。[[礫]]を土坑の中や外に配置した墓は特に'''配石墓'''と呼ばれる。
副葬品や人骨が出土しなくても、覆土の土壌分析とくに[[脂肪酸]]分析によって土坑墓と判別される場合がある。
==弥生時代の土坑墓の諸特徴== 基本的には縄文時代の諸特徴と共通するが、平面形状は楕円形や隅丸長方形などがあらわれる。[[伸展葬]]の普及により、年代が下ると長方形を呈するものが次第に増える。
==関連項目== *[[土坑]] *[[屈葬]] *[[木棺墓]] *[[甕棺墓]] *[[石棺墓]]
{{stub}}

revision.comment: 加筆
revision.text:
'''土坑墓'''（どこうぼ）とは、土を掘りくぼめて穴（[[土坑]]）をつくり、そこに人の遺体を納めて[[葬送]]した[[遺構]]。[[土葬]]にともなう世界的にポピュラーな埋葬に用いられた遺構であるが、日本では[[縄文時代]]～[[弥生時代]]に多い墓の形式であり、その場合「土壙」とか「土壙墓」と表記される場合もある。
==旧石器時代の土坑墓== [[大阪府]][[藤井寺市]]の[[はさみ山遺跡]]梨田地点から、墓と推定される楕円形の土坑が確認されている。
==縄文時代の土坑墓の諸特徴== #径はおおむね0.7～1.5mの範囲。平面形状は円形または方形で、多くは円形を呈する。 #深さは平面規模に比べて浅く、底が平坦である。 #[[覆土]]はたいていの場合、一括埋め戻しの人為堆積である。 #[[人骨]]をともなう場合もあるが、まったく痕跡をとどめない場合も多い。 #[[副葬品]]をともなう場合がある。後期・晩期になると質・量ともに充実し、特に晩期には工芸品的な価値の高いものが副葬される場合が多くなる。
縄文時代の葬送は[[貝塚]]などから見つかる人骨のようすから、[[屈葬]]が一般的だったと考えられる。規模や形状はそれを反映したものととらえることができる。[[礫]]を土坑の中や外に配置した墓は特に'''配石墓'''と呼ばれる。
副葬品や人骨が出土しなくても、覆土の土壌分析とくに[[脂肪酸]]分析によって土坑墓と判別される場合がある。
==弥生時代の土坑墓の諸特徴== 基本的には縄文時代の諸特徴と共通するが、平面形状は楕円形や隅丸長方形などがあらわれる。[[伸展葬]]の普及により、年代が下ると長方形を呈するものが次第に増える。
==関連項目== *[[土坑]] *[[屈葬]] *[[木棺墓]] *[[甕棺墓]] *[[石棺墓]] *[[弥生時代の墓制]] {{stub}}

'''土坑墓'''（どこうぼ）とは、土を掘りくぼめて穴（[[土坑]]）をつくり、そこに人の遺体を納めて[[葬送]]した[[遺構]]。[[土葬]]にともなう世界的にポピュラーな埋葬に用いられた遺構であるが、日本では[[縄文時代]]～[[弥生時代]]に多い墓の形式であり、その場合「土壙」とか「土壙墓」と表記される場合もある。
==旧石器時代の土坑墓== [[大阪府]][[藤井寺市]]の[[はさみ山遺跡]]梨田地点から、墓と推定される楕円形の土坑が確認されている。
==縄文時代の土坑墓の諸特徴== #径はおおむね0.7～1.5mの範囲。平面形状は円形または方形で、多くは円形を呈する。 #深さは平面規模に比べて浅く、底が平坦である。 #[[覆土]]はたいていの場合、一括埋め戻しの人為堆積である。 #[[人骨]]をともなう場合もあるが、まったく痕跡をとどめない場合も多い。 #[[副葬品]]をともなう場合がある。後期・晩期になると質・量ともに充実し、特に晩期には工芸品的な価値の高いものが副葬される場合が多くなる。
縄文時代の葬送は[[貝塚]]などから見つかる人骨のようすから、[[屈葬]]が一般的だったと考えられる。規模や形状はそれを反映したものととらえることができる。[[礫]]を土坑の中や外に配置した墓は特に'''配石墓'''と呼ばれる。
副葬品や人骨が出土しなくても、覆土の土壌分析とくに[[脂肪酸]]分析によって土坑墓と判別される場合がある。
==弥生時代の土坑墓の諸特徴== 基本的には縄文時代の諸特徴と共通するが、平面形状は楕円形や隅丸長方形などがあらわれる。[[伸展葬]]の普及により、年代が下ると長方形を呈するものが次第に増える。
==関連項目== *[[土坑]] *[[屈葬]] *[[木棺墓]] *[[甕棺墓]] *[[石棺墓]] *[[弥生時代の墓制]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とこうほ}} [[Category:縄文時代]] [[Category:弥生時代]] [[Category:遺構]] [[Category:葬送]]

'''土坑墓'''（どこうぼ）とは、土を掘りくぼめて穴（[[土坑]]）をつくり、そこに人の[[遺体]]を納めて[[葬送]]した[[遺構]]。[[土葬]]に伴う世界的にポピュラーな埋葬に用いられた遺構であるが、日本では[[縄文時代]]～[[弥生時代]]に多い墓の形式であり、その場合「'''土壙'''」とか「'''土壙墓'''」と表記される場合もある。
==旧石器時代の土坑墓== [[大阪府]][[藤井寺市]]の[[はさみ山遺跡]]梨田地点から、墓と推定される楕円形の土坑が確認されている。
==縄文時代の土坑墓の諸特徴== #径はおおむね0.7～1.5mの範囲。平面形状は円形または方形で、多くは円形を呈する。 #深さは平面規模に比べて浅く、底が平坦である。 #[[覆土 (考古学)|覆土]]はたいていの場合、一括埋め戻しの人為堆積である。 #[[人骨]]を伴う場合もあるが、まったく痕跡をとどめない場合も多い。 #[[副葬品]]を伴う場合がある。後期・晩期になると質・量ともに充実し、特に晩期には工芸品的な価値の高いものが副葬される場合が多くなる。
縄文時代の葬送は[[貝塚]]などから見つかる人骨のようすから、[[屈葬]]が一般的だったと考えられる。規模や形状はそれを反映したものととらえることができる。[[礫]]を土坑の中や外に配置した墓は特に'''配石墓'''と呼ばれる。
副葬品や人骨が出土しなくても、覆土の土壌分析とくに[[脂肪酸]]分析によって土坑墓と判別される場合がある。
==弥生時代の土坑墓の諸特徴== 基本的には縄文時代の諸特徴と共通するが、平面形状は楕円形や隅丸長方形などがあらわれる。[[伸展葬]]の普及により、年代が下ると長方形を呈するものが次第に増える。
==関連項目== *[[土坑]] *[[屈葬]] *[[木棺墓]] *[[甕棺墓]] *[[石棺墓]] *[[弥生時代の墓制]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とこうほ}} [[Category:縄文時代]] [[Category:弥生時代]] [[Category:遺構]] [[Category:葬儀]]

revision.text:
'''Earth-Pit Graves''' (土坑墓) refer to burial structures where {{ill|earth|ja|土砂}} is excavated to create a pit ({{ill|Earth Pit|ja|土坑}}) for interring human {{ill|remains|ja|遺体}}. These graves were commonly used worldwide for {{ill|burial|ja|埋葬}} practices associated with {{ill|in-ground burials|ja|土葬}}, and in Japan, they were a prevalent form of graves during the {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代}} to {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}}. In Japan, they are sometimes referred to as '''Earth Pits''' (土壙) or '''Earth-Pit Graves''' (土壙墓).
== Earth-Pit Graves of the Paleolithic Period == At the {{ill|Hasamiyama Site|ja|はさみ山遺跡}} in {{ill|Fujidera, Osaka|ja|藤井寺市}}, elliptical earth pits presumed to be graves have been identified.
== Characteristics of Jōmon Period Earth-Pit Graves == # The diameter typically ranges from 0.7 to 1.5 meters. The pits are circular or square in shape, with circular forms being most common. # The depth is shallow relative to the surface area, with flat bottoms. # {{ill|Cover soil|ja|覆土 (考古学)}} is usually backfilled manually in a single process. # While {{ill|human bones|ja|人骨}} are sometimes found, in many cases, no trace of them remains. # {{ill|Grave goods|ja|副葬品}} may accompany the burial. In the later and final phases of the period, the quality and quantity of such items increase, with many high-value crafted items being interred during the final phase.
Burial practices during the Jōmon period, inferred from human remains found in {{ill|shell middens|ja|貝塚}}, suggest that {{ill|flexed burial|ja|屈葬}} was common. The size and shape of the graves are thought to reflect this practice. Graves with {{ill|pebbles|ja|礫}} arranged inside or around the pits are specifically called '''Stone-Arranged Graves''' (配石墓).
Even when grave goods or human remains are absent, soil analyses, particularly of {{ill|fatty acid|ja|脂肪酸}} residues, can identify the structure as an earth-pit grave.
== Characteristics of Yayoi Period Earth-Pit Graves == The basic features remain similar to those of the Jōmon period, but new shapes, such as elliptical and rounded-rectangle forms, appear. With the increasing adoption of {{ill|extended burial|ja|伸展葬}} practices, rectangular-shaped pits become more prevalent in later periods.
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Earth Pit|ja|土坑}} * {{ill|Flexed Burial|ja|屈葬}} * {{ill|Wooden Coffin Graves|ja|木棺墓}} * {{ill|Jar Coffin Graves|ja|甕棺墓}} * {{ill|Stone Coffin Graves|ja|石棺墓}} * {{ill|Burial Practices of the Yayoi Period|ja|弥生時代の墓制}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}
[[Category:Jōmon Period]] [[Category:Yayoi Period]] [[Category:Archaeological Sites]] [[Category:Funerary Practices]] [[ja:土坑墓]]

With the increasing adoption of {{ill|extended burial|ja|伸展葬}} practices, rectangular-shaped pits become more prevalent in later periods.

revision.comment: deciding to rename this category to a more descriptive one
revision.text:
'''Earth-Pit Graves''' (土坑墓) refer to burial structures where {{ill|earth|ja|土砂}} is excavated to create a pit ([[:en:Prehistoric storage pits|Earth Pit]]) for interring human [[:en:corpse|remains]]. These graves were commonly used worldwide for {{ill|burial|ja|埋葬}} practices associated with {{ill|in-ground burials|ja|土葬}}, and in Japan, they were a prevalent form of graves during the {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代}} to {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}}. In Japan, they are sometimes referred to as '''Earth Pits''' (土壙) or '''Earth-Pit Graves''' (土壙墓).
== Earth-Pit Graves of the Paleolithic Period == At the {{ill|Hasamiyama Site|ja|はさみ山遺跡}} in {{ill|Fujidera, Osaka|ja|藤井寺市}}, elliptical earth pits presumed to be graves have been identified.
== Characteristics of Jōmon Period Earth-Pit Graves == # The diameter typically ranges from 0.7 to 1.5 meters. The pits are circular or square in shape, with circular forms being most common. # The depth is shallow relative to the surface area, with flat bottoms. # {{ill|Cover soil|ja|覆土 (考古学)}} is usually backfilled manually in a single process. # While {{ill|human bones|ja|人骨}} are sometimes found, in many cases, no trace of them remains. # {{ill|Grave goods|ja|副葬品}} may accompany the burial. In the later and final phases of the period, the quality and quantity of such items increase, with many high-value crafted items being interred during the final phase.
Burial practices during the Jōmon period, inferred from human remains found in {{ill|shell middens|ja|貝塚}}, suggest that {{ill|flexed burial|ja|屈葬}} was common. The size and shape of the graves are thought to reflect this practice. Graves with {{ill|pebbles|ja|礫}} arranged inside or around the pits are specifically called '''Stone-Arranged Graves''' (配石墓).
Even when grave goods or human remains are absent, soil analyses, particularly of {{ill|fatty acid|ja|脂肪酸}} residues, can identify the structure as an earth-pit grave.
== Characteristics of Yayoi Period Earth-Pit Graves == The basic features remain similar to those of the Jōmon period, but new shapes, such as elliptical and rounded-rectangle forms, appear. With the increasing adoption of {{ill|extended burial|ja|伸展葬}} practices, rectangular-shaped pits become more prevalent in later periods.
== Related Topics == * [[:en:Prehistoric storage pits|Earth Pit]] * {{ill|Flexed burial|ja|屈葬}} * {{ill|Wooden Coffin Graves|ja|木棺墓}} * {{ill|Jar Coffin Graves|ja|甕棺墓}} * {{ill|Stone Coffin Graves|ja|石棺墓}} * {{ill|Burial Practices of the Yayoi Period|ja|弥生時代の墓制}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

The pits are circular or square in shape, with circular forms being most common. # The depth is shallow relative to the surface area, with flat bottoms. # {{ill|Cover soil|ja|覆土 (考古学)}} is usually backfilled manually in a single process. # While {{ill|human bones|ja|人骨}} are sometimes found, in many cases, no trace of them remains. # {{ill|Grave goods|ja|副葬品}} may accompany the burial. In the later and final phases of the period, the quality and quantity of such items increase, with many high-value crafted items being interred during the final phase.
Burial practices during the Jōmon period, inferred from human remains found in {{ill|shell middens|ja|貝塚}}, suggest that {{ill|flexed burial|ja|屈葬}} was common. The size and shape of the graves are thought to reflect this practice. Graves with {{ill|pebbles|ja|礫}} arranged inside or around the pits are specifically called '''Stone-Arranged Graves''' (配石墓).
Even when grave goods or human remains are absent, soil analyses, particularly of {{ill|fatty acid|ja|脂肪酸}} residues, can identify the structure as an earth-pit grave.
== Characteristics of Yayoi Period Earth-Pit Graves == The basic features remain similar to those of the Jōmon period, but new shapes, such as elliptical and rounded-rectangle forms, appear. With the increasing adoption of {{ill|extended burial|ja|伸展葬}} practices, rectangular-shaped pits become more prevalent in later periods.

Even when grave goods or human remains are absent, soil analyses, particularly of {{ill|fatty acid|ja|脂肪酸}} residues, can identify the structure as an earth-pit grave.
== Characteristics of Yayoi Period Earth-Pit Graves == The basic features remain similar to those of the Jōmon period, but new shapes, such as elliptical and rounded-rectangle forms, appear. With the increasing adoption of {{ill|extended burial|ja|伸展葬}} practices, rectangular-shaped pits become more prevalent in later periods.

'''Earth-Pit Graves''' (土坑墓) refer to burial structures where {{ill|earth|ja|土砂}} is excavated to create a pit ([[:en:Prehistoric storage pits|Earth Pit]]) for interring human [[:en:corpse|remains]]. These graves were commonly used worldwide for {{ill|burial|ja|埋葬}} practices associated with {{ill|in-ground burials|ja|土葬}}, and in Japan, they were a prevalent form of graves during the {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代}} to {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}}. In Japan, they are sometimes referred to as '''Earth Pits''' (土壙) or '''Earth-Pit Graves''' (土壙墓).
== Earth-Pit Graves of the Paleolithic Period == At the {{ill|Hasamiyama Site|ja|はさみ山遺跡}} in {{ill|Fujidera, Osaka|ja|藤井寺市}}, elliptical earth pits presumed to be graves have been identified.
== Characteristics of Jōmon Period Earth-Pit Graves == # The diameter typically ranges from 0.7 to 1.5 meters. The pits are circular or square in shape, with circular forms being most common. # The depth is shallow relative to the surface area, with flat bottoms. # {{ill|Cover soil|ja|覆土 (考古学)}} is usually backfilled manually in a single process. # While {{ill|human bones|ja|人骨}} are sometimes found, in many cases, no trace of them remains. # {{ill|Grave goods|ja|副葬品}} may accompany the burial. In the later and final phases of the period, the quality and quantity of such items increase, with many high-value crafted items being interred during the final phase.
Burial practices during the Jōmon period, inferred from human remains found in {{ill|shell middens|ja|貝塚}}, suggest that {{ill|flexed burial|ja|屈葬}} was common. The size and shape of the graves are thought to reflect this practice. Graves with {{ill|pebbles|ja|礫}} arranged inside or around the pits are specifically called '''Stone-Arranged Graves''' (配石墓).
Even when grave goods or human remains are absent, soil analyses, particularly of {{ill|fatty acid|ja|脂肪酸}} residues, can identify the structure as an earth-pit grave.
== Characteristics of Yayoi Period Earth-Pit Graves == The basic features remain similar to those of the Jōmon period, but new shapes, such as elliptical and rounded-rectangle forms, appear. With the increasing adoption of {{ill|extended burial|ja|伸展葬}} practices, rectangular-shaped pits become more prevalent in later periods.
== Related Topics == * [[:en:Prehistoric storage pits|Earth Pit]] * {{ill|Flexed burial|ja|屈葬}} * {{ill|Wooden Coffin Graves|ja|木棺墓}} * {{ill|Jar Coffin Graves|ja|甕棺墓}} * {{ill|Stone Coffin Graves|ja|石棺墓}} * {{ill|Burial Practices of the Yayoi Period|ja|弥生時代の墓制}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

'''Earth-Pit Graves''' (土坑墓) refer to burial structures where {{ill|earth|ja|土砂}} is excavated to create a pit ([[:en:Prehistoric storage pits|Earth Pit]]) for interring human [[:en:corpse|remains]]. These graves were commonly used worldwide for {{ill|burial|ja|埋葬}} practices associated with {{ill|in-ground burials|ja|土葬}}, and in Japan, they were a prevalent form of graves during the {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代}} to {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}}. In Japan, they are sometimes referred to as '''Earth Pits''' (土壙) or '''Earth-Pit Graves''' (土壙墓).
== Earth-Pit Graves of the Paleolithic Period == At the {{ill|Hasamiyama Site|ja|はさみ山遺跡}} in {{ill|Fujidera, Osaka|ja|藤井寺市}}, elliptical earth pits presumed to be graves have been identified.
== Characteristics of Jōmon Period Earth-Pit Graves == # The diameter typically ranges from 0.7 to 1.5 meters. The pits are circular or square in shape, with circular forms being most common. # The depth is shallow relative to the surface area, with flat bottoms. # {{ill|Cover soil|ja|覆土 (考古学)}} is usually backfilled manually in a single process. # While {{ill|human bones|ja|人骨}} are sometimes found, in many cases, no trace of them remains. # {{ill|Grave goods|ja|副葬品}} may accompany the burial. In the later and final phases of the period, the quality and quantity of such items increase, with many high-value crafted items being interred during the final phase.
Burial practices during the Jōmon period, inferred from human remains found in {{ill|shell middens|ja|貝塚}}, suggest that {{ill|flexed burial|ja|屈葬}} was common. The size and shape of the graves are thought to reflect this practice. Graves with {{ill|pebbles|ja|礫}} arranged inside or around the pits are specifically called '''Stone-Arranged Graves''' (配石墓).
Even when grave goods or human remains are absent, soil analyses, particularly of {{ill|fatty acid|ja|脂肪酸}} residues, can identify the structure as an earth-pit grave.
== Characteristics of Yayoi Period Earth-Pit Graves == The basic features remain similar to those of the Jōmon period, but new shapes, such as elliptical and rounded-rectangle forms, appear. With the increasing adoption of {{ill|extended burial|ja|伸展葬}} practices, rectangular-shaped pits become more prevalent in later periods.

'''Earth-Pit Graves''' (土坑墓) refer to burial structures where {{ill|earth|ja|土砂}} is excavated to create a pit ([[:en:Prehistoric storage pits|Earth Pit]]) for interring human [[:en:corpse|remains]]. These graves were commonly used worldwide for {{ill|burial|ja|埋葬}} practices associated with {{ill|in-ground burials|ja|土葬}}, and in Japan, they were a prevalent form of graves during the {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代}} to {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}}. In Japan, they are sometimes referred to as '''Earth Pits''' (土壙) or '''Earth-Pit Graves''' (土壙墓).
== Earth-Pit Graves of the Paleolithic Period == At the {{ill|Hasamiyama Site|ja|はさみ山遺跡}} in {{ill|Fujidera, Osaka|ja|藤井寺市}}, elliptical earth pits presumed to be graves have been identified.
== Characteristics of Jōmon Period Earth-Pit Graves == # The diameter typically ranges from 0.7 to 1.5 meters. The pits are circular or square in shape, with circular forms being most common. # The depth is shallow relative to the surface area, with flat bottoms. # {{ill|Cover soil|ja|覆土 (考古学)}} is usually backfilled manually in a single process. # While {{ill|human bones|ja|人骨}} are sometimes found, in many cases, no trace of them remains. # {{ill|Grave goods|ja|副葬品}} may accompany the burial. In the later and final phases of the period, the quality and quantity of such items increase, with many high-value crafted items being interred during the final phase.
Burial practices during the Jōmon period, inferred from human remains found in {{ill|shell middens|ja|貝塚}}, suggest that {{ill|flexed burial|ja|屈葬}} was common. The size and shape of the graves are thought to reflect this practice. Graves with {{ill|pebbles|ja|礫}} arranged inside or around the pits are specifically called '''Stone-Arranged Graves''' (配石墓).
Even when grave goods or human remains are absent, soil analyses, particularly of {{ill|fatty acid|ja|脂肪酸}} residues, can identify the structure as an earth-pit grave.
== Characteristics of Yayoi Period Earth-Pit Graves == The basic features remain similar to those of the Jōmon period, but new shapes, such as elliptical and rounded-rectangle forms, appear. With the increasing adoption of {{ill|extended burial|ja|伸展葬}} practices, rectangular-shaped pits become more prevalent in later periods.
== Related Topics == * [[:en:Prehistoric storage pits|Earth Pit]] * {{ill|Flexed burial|ja|屈葬}} * {{ill|Wooden Coffin Graves|ja|木棺墓}} * {{ill|Jar Coffin Graves|ja|甕棺墓}} * {{ill|Stone Coffin Graves|ja|石棺墓}} * {{ill|Burial Practices of the Yayoi Period|ja|弥生時代の墓制|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Goodbye $1]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Earth-Pit Graves''' (土坑墓) refer to burial structures where {{ill|earth|ja|土砂}} is excavated to create a pit ([[:en:Prehistoric storage pits|Earth Pit]]) for interring human [[:en:corpse|remains]]. These graves were commonly used worldwide for {{ill|burial|ja|埋葬}} practices associated with {{ill|in-ground burials|ja|土葬}}, and in Japan, they were a prevalent form of graves during the {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代}} to {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}}. In Japan, they are sometimes referred to as '''Earth Pits''' (土壙) or '''Earth-Pit Graves''' (土壙墓).
== Earth-Pit Graves of the Paleolithic Period == At the {{ill|Hasamiyama Site|ja|はさみ山遺跡}} in {{ill|Fujidera, Osaka|ja|藤井寺市}}, elliptical earth pits presumed to be graves have been identified.
== Characteristics of Jōmon Period Earth-Pit Graves == # The diameter typically ranges from 0.7 to 1.5 meters. The pits are circular or square in shape, with circular forms being most common. # The depth is shallow relative to the surface area, with flat bottoms. # {{ill|Cover soil|ja|覆土 (考古学)}} is usually backfilled manually in a single process. # While {{ill|human bones|ja|人骨}} are sometimes found, in many cases, no trace of them remains. # {{ill|Grave goods|ja|副葬品}} may accompany the burial. In the later and final phases of the period, the quality and quantity of such items increase, with many high-value crafted items being interred during the final phase.
Burial practices during the Jōmon period, inferred from human remains found in {{ill|shell middens|ja|貝塚}}, suggest that {{ill|flexed burial|ja|屈葬}} was common. The size and shape of the graves are thought to reflect this practice. Graves with {{ill|pebbles|ja|礫}} arranged inside or around the pits are specifically called '''Stone-Arranged Graves''' (配石墓).
Even when grave goods or human remains are absent, soil analyses, particularly of {{ill|fatty acid|ja|脂肪酸}} residues, can identify the structure as an earth-pit grave.
== Characteristics of Yayoi Period Earth-Pit Graves == The basic features remain similar to those of the Jōmon period, but new shapes, such as elliptical and rounded-rectangle forms, appear. With the increasing adoption of {{ill|extended burial|ja|伸展葬}} practices, rectangular-shaped pits become more prevalent in later periods.
== See Also == * [[:en:Prehistoric storage pits|Earth Pit]] * {{ill|Flexed burial|ja|屈葬}} * {{ill|Wooden Coffin Graves|ja|木棺墓}} * {{ill|Jar Coffin Graves|ja|甕棺墓}} * {{ill|Stone Coffin Graves|ja|石棺墓}} * {{ill|Burial Practices of the Yayoi Period|ja|弥生時代の墓制|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

== Earth-Pit Graves of the Paleolithic Period == At the {{ill|Hasamiyama Site|ja|はさみ山遺跡}} in {{ill|Fujidera, Osaka|ja|藤井寺市}}, elliptical earth pits presumed to be graves have been identified.
== Characteristics of Jōmon Period Earth-Pit Graves == # The diameter typically ranges from 0.7 to 1.5 meters. The pits are circular or square in shape, with circular forms being most common. # The depth is shallow relative to the surface area, with flat bottoms. # {{ill|Cover soil|ja|覆土 (考古学)}} is usually backfilled manually in a single process. # While {{ill|human bones|ja|人骨}} are sometimes found, in many cases, no trace of them remains. # {{ill|Grave goods|ja|副葬品}} may accompany the burial. In the later and final phases of the period, the quality and quantity of such items increase, with many high-value crafted items being interred during the final phase.
Burial practices during the Jōmon period, inferred from human remains found in {{ill|shell middens|ja|貝塚}}, suggest that {{ill|flexed burial|ja|屈葬}} was common. The size and shape of the graves are thought to reflect this practice. Graves with {{ill|pebbles|ja|礫}} arranged inside or around the pits are specifically called '''Stone-Arranged Graves''' (配石墓).
Even when grave goods or human remains are absent, soil analyses, particularly of {{ill|fatty acid|ja|脂肪酸}} residues, can identify the structure as an earth-pit grave.
== Characteristics of Yayoi Period Earth-Pit Graves == The basic features remain similar to those of the Jōmon period, but new shapes, such as elliptical and rounded-rectangle forms, appear. With the increasing adoption of {{ill|extended burial|ja|伸展葬}} practices, rectangular-shaped pits become more prevalent in later periods.
== See Also == * [[:en:Prehistoric storage pits|Earth Pit]] * {{ill|Flexed burial|ja|屈葬}} * {{ill|Wooden Coffin Graves|ja|木棺墓}} * {{ill|Jar Coffin Graves|ja|甕棺墓}} * {{ill|Stone Coffin Graves|ja|石棺墓}} * {{ill|Burial Practices of the Yayoi Period|ja|弥生時代の墓制|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "↵↵== See Also ==" to "== See Also =="
revision.text:
'''Earth-Pit Graves''' (土坑墓) refer to burial structures where {{ill|earth|ja|土砂}} is excavated to create a pit ([[:en:Prehistoric storage pits|Earth Pit]]) for interring human [[:en:corpse|remains]]. These graves were commonly used worldwide for {{ill|burial|ja|埋葬}} practices associated with {{ill|in-ground burials|ja|土葬}}, and in Japan, they were a prevalent form of graves during the {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代}} to {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}}. In Japan, they are sometimes referred to as '''Earth Pits''' (土壙) or '''Earth-Pit Graves''' (土壙墓).
== Earth-Pit Graves of the Paleolithic Period == At the {{ill|Hasamiyama Site|ja|はさみ山遺跡}} in {{ill|Fujidera, Osaka|ja|藤井寺市}}, elliptical earth pits presumed to be graves have been identified.
== Characteristics of Jōmon Period Earth-Pit Graves == # The diameter typically ranges from 0.7 to 1.5 meters. The pits are circular or square in shape, with circular forms being most common. # The depth is shallow relative to the surface area, with flat bottoms. # {{ill|Cover soil|ja|覆土 (考古学)|1=Overburden (archaeology)|lt=Overburden}} is usually backfilled manually in a single process. # While {{ill|human bones|ja|人骨}} are sometimes found, in many cases, no trace of them remains. # {{ill|Grave goods|ja|副葬品}} may accompany the burial. In the later and final phases of the period, the quality and quantity of such items increase, with many high-value crafted items being interred during the final phase.
Burial practices during the Jōmon period, inferred from human remains found in {{ill|shell middens|ja|貝塚}}, suggest that {{ill|flexed burial|ja|屈葬}} was common. The size and shape of the graves are thought to reflect this practice. Graves with {{ill|pebbles|ja|礫}} arranged inside or around the pits are specifically called '''Stone-Arranged Graves''' (配石墓).
Even when grave goods or human remains are absent, soil analyses, particularly of {{ill|fatty acid|ja|脂肪酸}} residues, can identify the structure as an earth-pit grave.
== Characteristics of Yayoi Period Earth-Pit Graves == The basic features remain similar to those of the Jōmon period, but new shapes, such as elliptical and rounded-rectangle forms, appear. With the increasing adoption of {{ill|extended burial|ja|伸展葬}} practices, rectangular-shaped pits become more prevalent in later periods.== See Also == * [[:en:Prehistoric storage pits|Earth Pit]] * {{ill|Flexed burial|ja|屈葬}} * {{ill|Wooden Coffin Graves|ja|木棺墓}} * {{ill|Jar Coffin Graves|ja|甕棺墓}} * {{ill|Stone Coffin Graves|ja|石棺墓}} * {{ill|Burial Practices of the Yayoi Period|ja|弥生時代の墓制|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

'''Earth-Pit Graves''' (土坑墓) refer to burial structures where {{ill|earth|ja|土砂}} is excavated to create a pit ([[:en:Prehistoric storage pits|Earth Pit]]) for interring human [[:en:corpse|remains]]. revision.text:
'''Earth-Pit Graves''' (土坑墓) refer to burial structures where {{ill|earth|ja|土砂}} is excavated to create a pit ([[:en:Prehistoric storage pits|Earth Pit]]) for interring human [[:en:corpse|remains]]. These graves were commonly used worldwide for {{ill|burial|ja|埋葬}} practices associated with {{ill|in-ground burials|ja|土葬}}, and in Japan, they were a prevalent form of graves during the {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代}} to {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}}. In Japan, they are sometimes referred to as '''Earth Pits''' (土壙) or '''Earth-Pit Graves''' (土壙墓).
== Earth-Pit Graves of the Paleolithic Period == At the {{ill|Hasamiyama Site|ja|はさみ山遺跡}} in {{ill|Fujidera, Osaka|ja|藤井寺市}}, elliptical earth pits presumed to be graves have been identified.
== Characteristics of Jōmon Period Earth-Pit Graves == # The diameter typically ranges from 0.7 to 1.5 meters. The pits are circular or square in shape, with circular forms being most common. # The depth is shallow relative to the surface area, with flat bottoms. # {{ill|Cover soil|ja|覆土 (考古学)|1=Overburden (archaeology)|lt=Overburden}} is usually backfilled manually in a single process. # While {{ill|human bones|ja|人骨}} are sometimes found, in many cases, no trace of them remains. # {{ill|Grave goods|ja|副葬品}} may accompany the burial. In the later and final phases of the period, the quality and quantity of such items increase, with many high-value crafted items being interred during the final phase.
Burial practices during the Jōmon period, inferred from human remains found in {{ill|shell middens|ja|貝塚}}, suggest that {{ill|flexed burial|ja|屈葬}} was common. The size and shape of the graves are thought to reflect this practice. Graves with {{ill|pebbles|ja|礫}} arranged inside or around the pits are specifically called '''Stone-Arranged Graves''' (配石墓).
Even when grave goods or human remains are absent, soil analyses, particularly of {{ill|fatty acid|ja|脂肪酸}} residues, can identify the structure as an earth-pit grave.
== Characteristics of Yayoi Period Earth-Pit Graves == The basic features remain similar to those of the Jōmon period, but new shapes, such as elliptical and rounded-rectangle forms, appear. With the increasing adoption of {{ill|extended burial|ja|伸展葬}} practices, rectangular-shaped pits become more prevalent in later periods. == See Also == * [[:en:Prehistoric storage pits|Earth Pit]] * {{ill|Flexed burial|ja|屈葬}} * {{ill|Wooden Coffin Graves|ja|木棺墓}} * {{ill|Jar Coffin Graves|ja|甕棺墓}} * {{ill|Stone Coffin Graves|ja|石棺墓}} * {{ill|Burial Practices of the Yayoi Period|ja|弥生時代の墓制|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

'''Earth-Pit Graves''' (土坑墓) refer to burial structures where {{ill|earth|ja|土砂}} is excavated to create a pit ([[:en:Prehistoric storage pits|Earth Pit]]) for interring human [[:en:corpse|remains]]. These graves were commonly used worldwide for {{ill|burial|ja|埋葬}} practices associated with {{ill|in-ground burials|ja|土葬}}, and in Japan, they were a prevalent form of graves during the {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代}} to {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}}. In Japan, they are sometimes referred to as '''Earth Pits''' (土壙) or '''Earth-Pit Graves''' (土壙墓).
== Earth-Pit Graves of the Paleolithic Period == At the {{ill|Hasamiyama Site|ja|はさみ山遺跡}} in {{ill|Fujidera, Osaka|ja|藤井寺市}}, elliptical earth pits presumed to be graves have been identified.
== Characteristics of Jōmon Period Earth-Pit Graves == # The diameter typically ranges from 0.7 to 1.5 meters. The pits are circular or square in shape, with circular forms being most common. # The depth is shallow relative to the surface area, with flat bottoms. # {{ill|Cover soil|ja|覆土 (考古学)|1=Overburden (archaeology)|lt=Overburden}} is usually backfilled manually in a single process. # While {{ill|human bones|ja|人骨}} are sometimes found, in many cases, no trace of them remains. # {{ill|Grave goods|ja|副葬品}} may accompany the burial. In the later and final phases of the period, the quality and quantity of such items increase, with many high-value crafted items being interred during the final phase.
Burial practices during the Jōmon period, inferred from human remains found in {{ill|shell middens|ja|貝塚}}, suggest that {{ill|flexed burial|ja|屈葬}} was common. The size and shape of the graves are thought to reflect this practice. Graves with {{ill|pebbles|ja|礫}} arranged inside or around the pits are specifically called '''Stone-Arranged Graves''' (配石墓).
Even when grave goods or human remains are absent, soil analyses, particularly of {{ill|fatty acid|ja|脂肪酸}} residues, can identify the structure as an earth-pit grave.

With the increasing adoption of {{ill|extended burial|ja|伸展葬}} practices, rectangular-shaped pits become more prevalent in later periods. == See Also == * [[:en:Prehistoric storage pits|Earth Pit]] * {{ill|Flexed burial|ja|屈葬}} * {{ill|Wooden Coffin Graves|ja|木棺墓}} * {{ill|Jar Coffin Graves|ja|甕棺墓}} * {{ill|Stone Coffin Graves|ja|石棺墓}} * {{ill|Burial Practices of the Yayoi Period|ja|弥生時代の墓制|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period}}

With the increasing adoption of {{ill|extended burial|ja|伸展葬|12=draft}} practices, rectangular-shaped pits become more prevalent in later periods. == See Also == * [[:en:Prehistoric storage pits|Earth Pit]] * {{ill|Flexed burial|ja|屈葬|12=draft}} * {{ill|Wooden Coffin Graves|ja|木棺墓|12=draft}} * {{ill|Jar Coffin Graves|ja|甕棺墓|12=draft}} * {{ill|Stone Coffin Graves|ja|石棺墓|12=draft}} * {{ill|Burial Practices of the Yayoi Period|ja|弥生時代の墓制|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|12=draft}}

revision.text:
'''Earth-Pit Graves''' (土坑墓) refer to burial structures where {{ill|earth|ja|土砂|12=draft}} is excavated to create a pit ([[:en:Prehistoric storage pits|Earth Pit]]) for interring human [[:en:corpse|remains]]. These graves were commonly used worldwide for {{ill|burial|ja|埋葬|12=draft}} practices associated with {{ill|in-ground burials|ja|土葬|1=burial|lt=In-ground burials|12=draft}}, and in Japan, they were a prevalent form of graves during the {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代|12=draft}} to {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代|12=draft}}. In Japan, they are sometimes referred to as '''Earth Pits''' (土壙) or '''Earth-Pit Graves''' (土壙墓).
== Earth-Pit Graves of the Paleolithic Period == At the {{ill|Hasamiyama Site|ja|はさみ山遺跡|12=draft}} in {{ill|Fujidera, Osaka|ja|藤井寺市|12=draft}}, elliptical earth pits presumed to be graves have been identified.
== Characteristics of Jōmon Period Earth-Pit Graves == # The diameter typically ranges from 0.7 to 1.5 meters. The pits are circular or square in shape, with circular forms being most common. # The depth is shallow relative to the surface area, with flat bottoms. # {{ill|Cover soil|ja|覆土 (考古学)|1=Overburden (archaeology)|lt=Overburden|12=draft}} is usually backfilled manually in a single process. # While {{ill|human bones|ja|人骨|12=draft}} are sometimes found, in many cases, no trace of them remains. # {{ill|Grave goods|ja|副葬品|12=draft}} may accompany the burial. In the later and final phases of the period, the quality and quantity of such items increase, with many high-value crafted items being interred during the final phase.
Burial practices during the Jōmon period, inferred from human remains found in {{ill|shell middens|ja|貝塚|12=draft}}, suggest that {{ill|flexed burial|ja|屈葬|12=draft}} was common. The size and shape of the graves are thought to reflect this practice. Graves with {{ill|pebbles|ja|礫|12=draft}} arranged inside or around the pits are specifically called '''Stone-Arranged Graves''' (配石墓).
Even when grave goods or human remains are absent, soil analyses, particularly of {{ill|fatty acid|ja|脂肪酸|12=draft}} residues, can identify the structure as an earth-pit grave.
== Characteristics of Yayoi Period Earth-Pit Graves == The basic features remain similar to those of the Jōmon period, but new shapes, such as elliptical and rounded-rectangle forms, appear. With the increasing adoption of {{ill|extended burial|ja|伸展葬|12=draft}} practices, rectangular-shaped pits become more prevalent in later periods. == See Also == * [[:en:Prehistoric storage pits|Earth Pit]] * {{ill|Flexed burial|ja|屈葬|12=draft}} * {{ill|Wooden Coffin Graves|ja|木棺墓|12=draft}} * {{ill|Jar Coffin Graves|ja|甕棺墓|12=draft}} * {{ill|Stone Coffin Graves|ja|石棺墓|12=draft}} * {{ill|Burial Practices of the Yayoi Period|ja|弥生時代の墓制|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|12=draft}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}
[[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Pit graves]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Pit graves]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Jōmon Period]] [[Category:Yayoi Period]] [[Category:Archaeological Sites]] [[Category:Funerary Practices]] [[Category:Peripheral articles]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Pit graves]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:ality]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:qq]]{{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Pit graves]] [[ja:土坑墓]]

revision.text:
'''土坑墓'''（どこうぼ）とは、[[土砂|土]]を掘りくぼめて穴（[[土坑]]）をつくり、そこに人の[[遺体]]を納めて[[葬送]]した[[遺構]]。[[土葬]]に伴う世界的にポピュラーな[[埋葬]]に用いられた遺構であるが、日本では[[縄文時代]]～[[弥生時代]]に多い墓の形式であり、その場合「'''土壙'''」とか「'''土壙墓'''」と表記される場合もある。
==旧石器時代の土坑墓== [[大阪府]][[藤井寺市]]の[[はさみ山遺跡]]梨田地点から、墓と推定される楕円形の土坑が確認されている。
==縄文時代の土坑墓の諸特徴== #径はおおむね0.7～1.5mの範囲。平面形状は円形または方形で、多くは円形を呈する。 #深さは平面規模に比べて浅く、底が平坦である。 #[[覆土 (考古学)|覆土]]はたいていの場合、一括埋め戻しの人為堆積である。 #[[人骨]]を伴う場合もあるが、まったく痕跡をとどめない場合も多い。 #[[副葬品]]を伴う場合がある。後期・晩期になると質・量ともに充実し、特に晩期には工芸品的な価値の高いものが副葬される場合が多くなる。
縄文時代の葬送は[[貝塚]]などから見つかる人骨のようすから、[[屈葬]]が一般的だったと考えられる。規模や形状はそれを反映したものととらえることができる。[[礫]]を土坑の中や外に配置した墓は特に'''配石墓'''と呼ばれる。
副葬品や人骨が出土しなくても、覆土の土壌分析とくに[[脂肪酸]]分析によって土坑墓と判別される場合がある。
==弥生時代の土坑墓の諸特徴== 基本的には縄文時代の諸特徴と共通するが、平面形状は楕円形や隅丸長方形などがあらわれる。[[伸展葬]]の普及により、年代が下ると長方形を呈するものが次第に増える。
==関連項目== *[[土坑]] *[[屈葬]] *[[木棺墓]] *[[甕棺墓]] *[[石棺墓]] *[[弥生時代の墓制]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とこうほ}} [[Category:縄文時代]] [[Category:弥生時代]] [[Category:遺構]] [[Category:葬儀]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:土坑墓]]
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q10856121]]
Wikidata name: hole-shaped grave
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Earth-Pit Graves''' (土坑墓) refer to burial structures where {{ill|earth|ja|土砂|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/earth|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is excavated to create a pit ([[:en:Prehistoric storage pits|Earth Pit]]) for interring human [[:en:corpse|remains]]. These graves were commonly used worldwide for [[:en:Burial|burial]] practices associated with {{ill|in-ground burials|ja|土葬|1=earth burial|lt=Earth burial|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/in-ground burials|13=User:Immanuelle/burial|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423872}}, and in Japan, they were a prevalent form of graves during the [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]] to [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]]. In Japan, they are sometimes referred to as '''Earth Pits''' (土壙) or '''Earth-Pit Graves''' (土壙墓).

== Earth-Pit Graves of the Paleolithic Period ==
At the {{ill|Hasamiyama Site|ja|はさみ山遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasamiyama Site|qq=}} in [[:en:Fujiidera|Fujidera, Osaka]], elliptical earth pits presumed to be graves have been identified.

== Characteristics of Jōmon Period Earth-Pit Graves ==
# The diameter typically ranges from 0.7 to 1.5 meters. The pits are circular or square in shape, with circular forms being most common.
# The depth is shallow relative to the surface area, with flat bottoms.
# {{ill|Cover soil|ja|覆土 (考古学)|1=Overburden (archaeology)|lt=Overburden|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cover soil|13=User:Immanuelle/Overburden (archaeology)|qq=}} is usually backfilled manually in a single process.
# While {{ill|human bones|ja|人骨|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/human bones|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 人間の骨の一覧|1=list of bones of the human skeleton|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16368}} are sometimes found, in many cases, no trace of them remains.
# [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] may accompany the burial. In the later and final phases of the period, the quality and quantity of such items increase, with many high-value crafted items being interred during the final phase.

Burial practices during the Jōmon period, inferred from human remains found in [[:en:Midden|shell middens]], suggest that {{ill|flexed burial|ja|屈葬|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/flexed burial|qq=}} was common. The size and shape of the graves are thought to reflect this practice. Graves with [[:en:Pebble|pebbles]] arranged inside or around the pits are specifically called '''Stone-Arranged Graves''' (配石墓).

Even when grave goods or human remains are absent, soil analyses, particularly of [[:en:Fatty acid|fatty acid]] residues, can identify the structure as an earth-pit grave.

== Characteristics of Yayoi Period Earth-Pit Graves ==
The basic features remain similar to those of the Jōmon period, but new shapes, such as elliptical and rounded-rectangle forms, appear. With the increasing adoption of {{ill|Stretched burial|lt=Stretched burial|lt=Extended burial|ja|伸展葬|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/extended burial|qq=}} practices, rectangular-shaped pits become more prevalent in later periods.
== See Also ==
* [[:en:Prehistoric storage pits|Earth Pit]]
* {{ill|Flexed burial|ja|屈葬|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flexed burial|qq=}}
* {{ill|Wooden Coffin Graves|ja|木棺墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin Graves|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弥生時代の墓制|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487466}}
* [[:en:Jar burial|Jar burial]]
* {{ill|Stone Coffin Graves|ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|1=Sekkan (Sarcophagus)|lt=Sarcophagus|qq=墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Coffin Graves|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekkan|qq=}}
* {{ill|Burial Practices of the Yayoi Period|ja|弥生時代の墓制|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Burial Practices of the Yayoi Period|13=User:Immanuelle/Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|qq=}}

{{Japanese-history-stub}}

In Japan, they are sometimes referred to as '''Earth Pits''' (土壙) or '''Earth-Pit Graves''' (土壙墓).

== Earth-Pit Graves of the Paleolithic Period ==
At the {{ill|Hasamiyama Site|ja|はさみ山遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasamiyama Site|qq=}} in [[:en:Fujiidera|Fujidera, Osaka]], elliptical earth pits presumed to be graves have been identified.

== Characteristics of Jōmon Period Earth-Pit Graves ==
# The diameter typically ranges from 0.7 to 1.5 meters. The pits are circular or square in shape, with circular forms being most common.
# The depth is shallow relative to the surface area, with flat bottoms.
# {{ill|Cover soil|ja|覆土 (考古学)|1=Overburden (archaeology)|lt=Overburden|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cover soil|13=User:Immanuelle/Overburden (archaeology)|qq=}} is usually backfilled manually in a single process.
# While {{ill|human bones|ja|人骨|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/human bones|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 人間の骨の一覧|1=list of bones of the human skeleton|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16368}} are sometimes found, in many cases, no trace of them remains.
# [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] may accompany the burial. In the later and final phases of the period, the quality and quantity of such items increase, with many high-value crafted items being interred during the final phase.

Burial practices during the Jōmon period, inferred from human remains found in [[:en:Midden|shell middens]], suggest that {{ill|flexed burial|ja|屈葬|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/flexed burial|qq=}} was common. The size and shape of the graves are thought to reflect this practice. Graves with [[:en:Pebble|pebbles]] arranged inside or around the pits are specifically called '''Stone-Arranged Graves''' (配石墓).

Even when grave goods or human remains are absent, soil analyses, particularly of [[:en:Fatty acid|fatty acid]] residues, can identify the structure as an earth-pit grave.

== Characteristics of Yayoi Period Earth-Pit Graves ==
The basic features remain similar to those of the Jōmon period, but new shapes, such as elliptical and rounded-rectangle forms, appear. With the increasing adoption of {{ill|Stretched burial|lt=Stretched burial|lt=Extended burial|ja|伸展葬|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/extended burial|qq=}} practices, rectangular-shaped pits become more prevalent in later periods.

* {{ill|Wooden Coffin Graves|ja|木棺墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin Graves|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弥生時代の墓制|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487466}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:土坑墓]]
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q10856121]]
Wikidata name: hole-shaped grave
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Earth-Pit Graves''' (土坑墓) refer to burial structures where {{ill|earth|ja|土砂|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/earth|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is excavated to create a pit ([[:en:Prehistoric storage pits|Earth Pit]]) for interring human [[:en:corpse|remains]]. These graves were commonly used worldwide for [[:en:Burial|burial]] practices associated with {{ill|in-ground burials|ja|土葬|1=earth burial|lt=Earth burial|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/in-ground burials|13=User:Immanuelle/burial|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423872}}, and in Japan, they were a prevalent form of graves during the [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]] to [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]]. In Japan, they are sometimes referred to as '''Earth Pits''' (土壙) or '''Earth-Pit Graves''' (土壙墓).

== Earth-Pit Graves of the Paleolithic Period ==
At the {{ill|Hasamiyama Site|ja|はさみ山遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasamiyama Site|qq=}} in [[:en:Fujiidera|Fujidera, Osaka]], elliptical earth pits presumed to be graves have been identified.

== Characteristics of Jōmon Period Earth-Pit Graves ==
# The diameter typically ranges from 0.7 to 1.5 meters. The pits are circular or square in shape, with circular forms being most common.
# The depth is shallow relative to the surface area, with flat bottoms.
# {{ill|Cover soil|ja|覆土 (考古学)|1=Overburden (archaeology)|lt=Overburden|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cover soil|13=User:Immanuelle/Overburden (archaeology)|qq=}} is usually backfilled manually in a single process.
# While {{ill|human bones|ja|人骨|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/human bones|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 人間の骨の一覧|1=list of bones of the human skeleton|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16368}} are sometimes found, in many cases, no trace of them remains.
# [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] may accompany the burial. In the later and final phases of the period, the quality and quantity of such items increase, with many high-value crafted items being interred during the final phase.

Burial practices during the Jōmon period, inferred from human remains found in [[:en:Midden|shell middens]], suggest that {{ill|flexed burial|ja|屈葬|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/flexed burial|qq=}} was common. The size and shape of the graves are thought to reflect this practice. Graves with [[:en:Pebble|pebbles]] arranged inside or around the pits are specifically called '''Stone-Arranged Graves''' (配石墓).

Even when grave goods or human remains are absent, soil analyses, particularly of [[:en:Fatty acid|fatty acid]] residues, can identify the structure as an earth-pit grave.

== Characteristics of Yayoi Period Earth-Pit Graves ==
The basic features remain similar to those of the Jōmon period, but new shapes, such as elliptical and rounded-rectangle forms, appear. With the increasing adoption of {{ill|Stretched burial|lt=Stretched burial|lt=Extended burial|ja|伸展葬|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/extended burial|qq=}} practices, rectangular-shaped pits become more prevalent in later periods.
== See Also ==
* [[:en:Prehistoric storage pits|Earth Pit]]
* {{ill|Flexed burial|ja|屈葬|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flexed burial|qq=}}
* {{ill|Wooden Coffin Graves|ja|木棺墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin Graves|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弥生時代の墓制|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487466}}
* [[:en:Jar burial|Jar burial]]
* {{ill|Stone Coffin Graves|ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|1=Sekkan (Sarcophagus)|lt=Sarcophagus|qq=墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Coffin Graves|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekkan|qq=}}
* {{ill|Burial Practices of the Yayoi Period|ja|弥生時代の墓制|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Burial Practices of the Yayoi Period|13=User:Immanuelle/Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|qq=}}

The size and shape of the graves are thought to reflect this practice. Graves with [[:en:Pebble|pebbles]] arranged inside or around the pits are specifically called '''Stone-Arranged Graves''' (配石墓).
Even when grave goods or human remains are absent, soil analyses, particularly of [[:en:Fatty acid|fatty acid]] residues, can identify the structure as an earth-pit grave.
== Characteristics of Yayoi Period Earth-Pit Graves == The basic features remain similar to those of the Jōmon period, but new shapes, such as elliptical and rounded-rectangle forms, appear. With the increasing adoption of {{ill|Stretched burial|lt=Stretched burial|lt=Extended burial|ja|伸展葬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/extended burial|qq=}} practices, rectangular-shaped pits become more prevalent in later periods. == See Also == * [[:en:Prehistoric storage pits|Earth Pit]] * {{ill|Flexed burial|ja|屈葬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flexed burial|qq=}} * {{ill|Wooden Coffin Graves|ja|木棺墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin Graves|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弥生時代の墓制|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487466}} * [[:en:Jar burial|Jar burial]] * {{ill|Stone Coffin Graves|ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|1=Sekkan (Sarcophagus)|lt=Sarcophagus|qq=墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Coffin Graves|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekkan|qq=}} * {{ill|Burial Practices of the Yayoi Period|ja|弥生時代の墓制|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Burial Practices of the Yayoi Period|13=User:Immanuelle/Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|qq=}}

These graves were commonly used worldwide for [[:en:Burial|burial]] practices associated with {{ill|in-ground burials|ja|土葬|1=earth burial|lt=Earth burial|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/in-ground burials|13=User:Immanuelle/burial|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423872}}, and in Japan, they were a prevalent form of graves during the [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]] to [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]]. In Japan, they are sometimes referred to as '''Earth Pits''' (土壙) or '''Earth-Pit Graves''' (土壙墓).
== Earth-Pit Graves of the Paleolithic Period == At the {{ill|Hasamiyama Site|ja|はさみ山遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasamiyama Site|qq=}} in [[:en:Fujiidera|Fujidera, Osaka]], elliptical earth pits presumed to be graves have been identified.
== Characteristics of Jōmon Period Earth-Pit Graves == # The diameter typically ranges from 0.7 to 1.5 meters. The pits are circular or square in shape, with circular forms being most common. # The depth is shallow relative to the surface area, with flat bottoms. # {{ill|Cover soil|ja|覆土 (考古学)|1=Overburden (archaeology)|lt=Overburden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cover soil|13=User:Immanuelle/Overburden (archaeology)|qq=}} is usually backfilled manually in a single process. # While {{ill|human bones|ja|人骨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/human bones|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 人間の骨の一覧|1=list of bones of the human skeleton|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16368}} are sometimes found, in many cases, no trace of them remains. # [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] may accompany the burial. In the later and final phases of the period, the quality and quantity of such items increase, with many high-value crafted items being interred during the final phase.
Burial practices during the Jōmon period, inferred from human remains found in [[:en:Midden|shell middens]], suggest that {{ill|flexed burial|ja|屈葬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/flexed burial|qq=}} was common. The size and shape of the graves are thought to reflect this practice. Graves with [[:en:Pebble|pebbles]] arranged inside or around the pits are specifically called '''Stone-Arranged Graves''' (配石墓).
Even when grave goods or human remains are absent, soil analyses, particularly of [[:en:Fatty acid|fatty acid]] residues, can identify the structure as an earth-pit grave.
== Characteristics of Yayoi Period Earth-Pit Graves == The basic features remain similar to those of the Jōmon period, but new shapes, such as elliptical and rounded-rectangle forms, appear. With the increasing adoption of {{ill|Stretched burial|lt=Stretched burial|lt=Extended burial|ja|伸展葬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/extended burial|qq=}} practices, rectangular-shaped pits become more prevalent in later periods. == See Also == * [[:en:Prehistoric storage pits|Earth Pit]] * {{ill|Flexed burial|ja|屈葬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flexed burial|qq=}} * {{ill|Wooden Coffin Graves|ja|木棺墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin Graves|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弥生時代の墓制|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487466}} * [[:en:Jar burial|Jar burial]] * {{ill|Stone Coffin Graves|ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|1=Sekkan (Sarcophagus)|lt=Sarcophagus|qq=墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Coffin Graves|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekkan|qq=}} * {{ill|Burial Practices of the Yayoi Period|ja|弥生時代の墓制|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Burial Practices of the Yayoi Period|13=User:Immanuelle/Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|qq=}}

revision.comment: Bot: add interwikis from Wikidata
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:土坑墓]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:土坑墓]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q10856121]] Wikidata name: hole-shaped grave [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Earth-Pit Graves''' (土坑墓) refer to burial structures where {{ill|earth|ja|土砂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/earth|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is excavated to create a pit ([[:en:Prehistoric storage pits|Earth Pit]]) for interring human [[:en:corpse|remains]]. These graves were commonly used worldwide for [[:en:Burial|burial]] practices associated with {{ill|in-ground burials|ja|土葬|1=earth burial|lt=Earth burial|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/in-ground burials|13=User:Immanuelle/burial|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423872}}, and in Japan, they were a prevalent form of graves during the [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]] to [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]]. In Japan, they are sometimes referred to as '''Earth Pits''' (土壙) or '''Earth-Pit Graves''' (土壙墓).
== Earth-Pit Graves of the Paleolithic Period == At the {{ill|Hasamiyama Site|ja|はさみ山遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasamiyama Site|qq=}} in [[:en:Fujiidera|Fujidera, Osaka]], elliptical earth pits presumed to be graves have been identified.
== Characteristics of Jōmon Period Earth-Pit Graves == # The diameter typically ranges from 0.7 to 1.5 meters. The pits are circular or square in shape, with circular forms being most common. # The depth is shallow relative to the surface area, with flat bottoms. # {{ill|Cover soil|ja|覆土 (考古学)|1=Overburden (archaeology)|lt=Overburden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cover soil|13=User:Immanuelle/Overburden (archaeology)|qq=}} is usually backfilled manually in a single process. # While {{ill|human bones|ja|人骨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/human bones|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 人間の骨の一覧|1=list of bones of the human skeleton|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16368}} are sometimes found, in many cases, no trace of them remains. # [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] may accompany the burial. In the later and final phases of the period, the quality and quantity of such items increase, with many high-value crafted items being interred during the final phase.
Burial practices during the Jōmon period, inferred from human remains found in [[:en:Midden|shell middens]], suggest that {{ill|flexed burial|ja|屈葬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/flexed burial|qq=}} was common. The size and shape of the graves are thought to reflect this practice. Graves with [[:en:Pebble|pebbles]] arranged inside or around the pits are specifically called '''Stone-Arranged Graves''' (配石墓).
Even when grave goods or human remains are absent, soil analyses, particularly of [[:en:Fatty acid|fatty acid]] residues, can identify the structure as an earth-pit grave.
== Characteristics of Yayoi Period Earth-Pit Graves == The basic features remain similar to those of the Jōmon period, but new shapes, such as elliptical and rounded-rectangle forms, appear. With the increasing adoption of {{ill|Stretched burial|lt=Stretched burial|lt=Extended burial|ja|伸展葬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/extended burial|qq=}} practices, rectangular-shaped pits become more prevalent in later periods. == See Also == * [[:en:Prehistoric storage pits|Earth Pit]] * {{ill|Flexed burial|ja|屈葬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flexed burial|qq=}} * {{ill|Wooden Coffin Graves|ja|木棺墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin Graves|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弥生時代の墓制|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487466}} * [[:en:Jar burial|Jar burial]] * {{ill|Stone Coffin Graves|ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|1=Sekkan (Sarcophagus)|lt=Sarcophagus|qq=墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Coffin Graves|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekkan|qq=}} * {{ill|Burial Practices of the Yayoi Period|ja|弥生時代の墓制|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Burial Practices of the Yayoi Period|13=User:Immanuelle/Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|qq=}}

With the increasing adoption of {{ill|Stretched burial|lt=Stretched burial|lt=Extended burial|ja|伸展葬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/extended burial|qq=}} practices, rectangular-shaped pits become more prevalent in later periods. == See Also == * [[:en:Prehistoric storage pits|Earth Pit]] * {{ill|Flexed burial|ja|屈葬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flexed burial|qq=}} * {{ill|Wooden Coffin Graves|ja|木棺墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin Graves|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弥生時代の墓制|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487466}} * [[:en:Jar burial|Jar burial]] * {{ill|Stone Coffin Graves|ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|1=Sekkan (Sarcophagus)|lt=Sarcophagus|qq=墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Coffin Graves|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekkan|qq=}} * {{ill|Burial Practices of the Yayoi Period|ja|弥生時代の墓制|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Burial Practices of the Yayoi Period|13=User:Immanuelle/Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|qq=}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:土坑墓]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:土坑墓]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:土坑墓]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q10856121]] Wikidata name: hole-shaped grave [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Earth-Pit Graves''' (土坑墓) refer to burial structures where {{ill|earth|ja|土砂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/earth|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is excavated to create a pit ([[:en:Prehistoric storage pits|Earth Pit]]) for interring human [[:en:corpse|remains]]. These graves were commonly used worldwide for [[:en:Burial|burial]] practices associated with {{ill|in-ground burials|ja|土葬|1=earth burial|lt=Earth burial|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/in-ground burials|13=User:Immanuelle/burial|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423872}}, and in Japan, they were a prevalent form of graves during the [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]] to [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]]. In Japan, they are sometimes referred to as '''Earth Pits''' (土壙) or '''Earth-Pit Graves''' (土壙墓).
== Earth-Pit Graves of the Paleolithic Period == At the {{ill|Hasamiyama Site|ja|はさみ山遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasamiyama Site|qq=}} in [[:en:Fujiidera|Fujidera, Osaka]], elliptical earth pits presumed to be graves have been identified.
== Characteristics of Jōmon Period Earth-Pit Graves == # The diameter typically ranges from 0.7 to 1.5 meters. The pits are circular or square in shape, with circular forms being most common. # The depth is shallow relative to the surface area, with flat bottoms. # {{ill|Cover soil|ja|覆土 (考古学)|1=Overburden (archaeology)|lt=Overburden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cover soil|13=User:Immanuelle/Overburden (archaeology)|qq=}} is usually backfilled manually in a single process. # While {{ill|human bones|ja|人骨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/human bones|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 人間の骨の一覧|1=list of bones of the human skeleton|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16368}} are sometimes found, in many cases, no trace of them remains. # [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] may accompany the burial. In the later and final phases of the period, the quality and quantity of such items increase, with many high-value crafted items being interred during the final phase.
Burial practices during the Jōmon period, inferred from human remains found in [[:en:Midden|shell middens]], suggest that {{ill|flexed burial|ja|屈葬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/flexed burial|qq=}} was common. The size and shape of the graves are thought to reflect this practice. Graves with [[:en:Pebble|pebbles]] arranged inside or around the pits are specifically called '''Stone-Arranged Graves''' (配石墓).
Even when grave goods or human remains are absent, soil analyses, particularly of [[:en:Fatty acid|fatty acid]] residues, can identify the structure as an earth-pit grave.
== Characteristics of Yayoi Period Earth-Pit Graves == The basic features remain similar to those of the Jōmon period, but new shapes, such as elliptical and rounded-rectangle forms, appear. With the increasing adoption of {{ill|Stretched burial|lt=Stretched burial|lt=Extended burial|ja|伸展葬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/extended burial|qq=}} practices, rectangular-shaped pits become more prevalent in later periods. == See Also == * [[:en:Prehistoric storage pits|Earth Pit]] * {{ill|Flexed burial|ja|屈葬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flexed burial|qq=}} * {{ill|Wooden Coffin Graves|ja|木棺墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin Graves|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弥生時代の墓制|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487466}} * [[:en:Jar burial|Jar burial]] * {{ill|Stone Coffin Graves|ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|1=Sekkan (Sarcophagus)|lt=Sarcophagus|qq=墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Coffin Graves|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekkan|qq=}} * {{ill|Burial Practices of the Yayoi Period|ja|弥生時代の墓制|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Burial Practices of the Yayoi Period|13=User:Immanuelle/Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|qq=}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

These graves were commonly used worldwide for {{ill|Burial|en|Burial|WD=Q331055|lt=burial}} practices associated with {{ill|in-ground burials|ja|土葬|1=earth burial|lt=Earth burial|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/in-ground burials|13=User:Immanuelle/burial|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423872}}, and in Japan, they were a prevalent form of graves during the {{ill|Jōmon period|en|Jōmon period|WD=Q52824|lt=Jōmon period}} to {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period}}. In Japan, they are sometimes referred to as '''Earth Pits''' (土壙) or '''Earth-Pit Graves''' (土壙墓).
== Earth-Pit Graves of the Paleolithic Period == At the {{ill|Hasamiyama Site|ja|はさみ山遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasamiyama Site|qq=}} in {{ill|Fujiidera|en|Fujiidera|WD=Q634011|lt=Fujidera, Osaka}}, elliptical earth pits presumed to be graves have been identified.
== Characteristics of Jōmon Period Earth-Pit Graves == # The diameter typically ranges from 0.7 to 1.5 meters. The pits are circular or square in shape, with circular forms being most common. # The depth is shallow relative to the surface area, with flat bottoms. # {{ill|Cover soil|ja|覆土 (考古学)|1=Overburden (archaeology)|lt=Overburden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cover soil|13=User:Immanuelle/Overburden (archaeology)|qq=}} is usually backfilled manually in a single process. # While {{ill|human bones|ja|人骨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/human bones|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 人間の骨の一覧|1=list of bones of the human skeleton|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16368}} are sometimes found, in many cases, no trace of them remains. # {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} may accompany the burial. In the later and final phases of the period, the quality and quantity of such items increase, with many high-value crafted items being interred during the final phase.
Burial practices during the Jōmon period, inferred from human remains found in {{ill|Midden|en|Midden|WD=Q1152199|lt=shell middens}}, suggest that {{ill|flexed burial|ja|屈葬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/flexed burial|qq=}} was common. The size and shape of the graves are thought to reflect this practice. Graves with {{ill|Pebble|en|Pebble|WD=Q14673|lt=pebbles}} arranged inside or around the pits are specifically called '''Stone-Arranged Graves''' (配石墓).
Even when grave goods or human remains are absent, soil analyses, particularly of {{ill|Fatty acid|en|Fatty acid|WD=Q61476|lt=fatty acid}} residues, can identify the structure as an earth-pit grave.
== Characteristics of Yayoi Period Earth-Pit Graves == The basic features remain similar to those of the Jōmon period, but new shapes, such as elliptical and rounded-rectangle forms, appear. With the increasing adoption of {{ill|Stretched burial|lt=Stretched burial|lt=Extended burial|ja|伸展葬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/extended burial|qq=}} practices, rectangular-shaped pits become more prevalent in later periods. == See Also == * {{ill|Prehistoric storage pits|en|Prehistoric storage pits|WD=Q1421012|lt=Earth Pit}} * {{ill|Flexed burial|ja|屈葬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flexed burial|qq=}} * {{ill|Wooden Coffin Graves|ja|木棺墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin Graves|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弥生時代の墓制|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487466}} * {{ill|Jar burial|en|Jar burial|WD=Q4244233|lt=Jar burial}} * {{ill|Stone Coffin Graves|ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|1=Sekkan (Sarcophagus)|lt=Sarcophagus|qq=墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Coffin Graves|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekkan|qq=}} * {{ill|Burial Practices of the Yayoi Period|ja|弥生時代の墓制|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Burial Practices of the Yayoi Period|13=User:Immanuelle/Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|qq=}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:土坑墓]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:土坑墓]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:土坑墓]]
'''Earth-Pit Graves''' (土坑墓) refer to burial structures where {{ill|earth|ja|土砂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/earth|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is excavated to create a pit ({{ill|Prehistoric storage pits|en|Prehistoric storage pits|WD=Q1421012|lt=Earth Pit}}) for interring human [[:en:corpse|remains]]. These graves were commonly used worldwide for {{ill|Burial|en|Burial|WD=Q331055|lt=burial}} practices associated with {{ill|in-ground burials|ja|土葬|1=earth burial|lt=Earth burial|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/in-ground burials|13=User:Immanuelle/burial|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423872}}, and in Japan, they were a prevalent form of graves during the {{ill|Jōmon period|en|Jōmon period|WD=Q52824|lt=Jōmon period}} to {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period}}. In Japan, they are sometimes referred to as '''Earth Pits''' (土壙) or '''Earth-Pit Graves''' (土壙墓).
== Earth-Pit Graves of the Paleolithic Period == At the {{ill|Hasamiyama Site|ja|はさみ山遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasamiyama Site|qq=}} in {{ill|Fujiidera|en|Fujiidera|WD=Q634011|lt=Fujidera, Osaka}}, elliptical earth pits presumed to be graves have been identified.
== Characteristics of Jōmon Period Earth-Pit Graves == # The diameter typically ranges from 0.7 to 1.5 meters. The pits are circular or square in shape, with circular forms being most common. # The depth is shallow relative to the surface area, with flat bottoms. # {{ill|Cover soil|ja|覆土 (考古学)|1=Overburden (archaeology)|lt=Overburden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cover soil|13=User:Immanuelle/Overburden (archaeology)|qq=}} is usually backfilled manually in a single process. # While {{ill|human bones|ja|人骨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/human bones|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 人間の骨の一覧|1=list of bones of the human skeleton|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16368}} are sometimes found, in many cases, no trace of them remains. # {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} may accompany the burial. In the later and final phases of the period, the quality and quantity of such items increase, with many high-value crafted items being interred during the final phase.
Burial practices during the Jōmon period, inferred from human remains found in {{ill|Midden|en|Midden|WD=Q1152199|lt=shell middens}}, suggest that {{ill|flexed burial|ja|屈葬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/flexed burial|qq=}} was common. The size and shape of the graves are thought to reflect this practice. Graves with {{ill|Pebble|en|Pebble|WD=Q14673|lt=pebbles}} arranged inside or around the pits are specifically called '''Stone-Arranged Graves''' (配石墓).
Even when grave goods or human remains are absent, soil analyses, particularly of {{ill|Fatty acid|en|Fatty acid|WD=Q61476|lt=fatty acid}} residues, can identify the structure as an earth-pit grave.
== Characteristics of Yayoi Period Earth-Pit Graves == The basic features remain similar to those of the Jōmon period, but new shapes, such as elliptical and rounded-rectangle forms, appear. With the increasing adoption of {{ill|Stretched burial|lt=Stretched burial|lt=Extended burial|ja|伸展葬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/extended burial|qq=}} practices, rectangular-shaped pits become more prevalent in later periods. == See Also == * {{ill|Prehistoric storage pits|en|Prehistoric storage pits|WD=Q1421012|lt=Earth Pit}} * {{ill|Flexed burial|ja|屈葬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flexed burial|qq=}} * {{ill|Wooden Coffin Graves|ja|木棺墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin Graves|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弥生時代の墓制|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487466}} * {{ill|Jar burial|en|Jar burial|WD=Q4244233|lt=Jar burial}} * {{ill|Stone Coffin Graves|ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|1=Sekkan (Sarcophagus)|lt=Sarcophagus|qq=墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Coffin Graves|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekkan|qq=}} * {{ill|Burial Practices of the Yayoi Period|ja|弥生時代の墓制|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Burial Practices of the Yayoi Period|13=User:Immanuelle/Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|qq=}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:土坑墓]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:土坑墓]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:土坑墓]]

'''Earth-Pit Graves''' (土坑墓) refer to burial structures where {{ill|earth|ja|土砂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/earth|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is excavated to create a pit ({{ill|Prehistoric storage pits|en|Prehistoric storage pits|WD=Q1421012|lt=Earth Pit|1=Prehistoric storage pits}}) for interring human [[:en:corpse|remains]]. These graves were commonly used worldwide for {{ill|Burial|en|Burial|WD=Q331055|lt=burial|1=Burial}} practices associated with {{ill|in-ground burials|ja|土葬|1=Earth burial|lt=Earth burial|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/in-ground burials|13=User:Immanuelle/burial|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423872}}, and in Japan, they were a prevalent form of graves during the {{ill|Jōmon period|en|Jōmon period|WD=Q52824|lt=Jōmon period|1=Jōmon period}} to {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}}. In Japan, they are sometimes referred to as '''Earth Pits''' (土壙) or '''Earth-Pit Graves''' (土壙墓).

== Earth-Pit Graves of the Paleolithic Period ==
At the {{ill|Hasamiyama Site|ja|はさみ山遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasamiyama Site|qq=}} in {{ill|Fujiidera|en|Fujiidera|WD=Q634011|lt=Fujidera, Osaka|1=Fujiidera}}, elliptical earth pits presumed to be graves have been identified.

== Characteristics of Jōmon Period Earth-Pit Graves ==
# The diameter typically ranges from 0.7 to 1.5 meters. The pits are circular or square in shape, with circular forms being most common.
# The depth is shallow relative to the surface area, with flat bottoms.
# {{ill|Cover soil|ja|覆土 (考古学)|1=Overburden (archaeology)|lt=Overburden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cover soil|13=User:Immanuelle/Overburden (archaeology)|qq=}} is usually backfilled manually in a single process.
# While {{ill|human bones|ja|人骨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/human bones|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 人間の骨の一覧|1=List of bones of the human skeleton|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16368}} are sometimes found, in many cases, no trace of them remains.
# {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} may accompany the burial. In the later and final phases of the period, the quality and quantity of such items increase, with many high-value crafted items being interred during the final phase.

Burial practices during the Jōmon period, inferred from human remains found in {{ill|Midden|en|Midden|WD=Q1152199|lt=shell middens|1=Midden}}, suggest that {{ill|flexed burial|ja|屈葬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/flexed burial|qq=}} was common. The size and shape of the graves are thought to reflect this practice. Graves with {{ill|Pebble|en|Pebble|WD=Q14673|lt=pebbles|1=Pebble}} arranged inside or around the pits are specifically called '''Stone-Arranged Graves''' (配石墓).

Even when grave goods or human remains are absent, soil analyses, particularly of {{ill|Fatty acid|en|Fatty acid|WD=Q61476|lt=fatty acid|1=Fatty acid}} residues, can identify the structure as an earth-pit grave.

== Characteristics of Yayoi Period Earth-Pit Graves ==
The basic features remain similar to those of the Jōmon period, but new shapes, such as elliptical and rounded-rectangle forms, appear. With the increasing adoption of {{ill|Stretched burial|lt=Stretched burial|lt=Extended burial|ja|伸展葬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/extended burial|qq=}} practices, rectangular-shaped pits become more prevalent in later periods.
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Prehistoric storage pits|en|Prehistoric storage pits|WD=Q1421012|lt=Earth Pit|1=Prehistoric storage pits}}
* {{ill|Flexed burial|ja|屈葬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flexed burial|qq=}}
* {{ill|Wooden Coffin Graves|ja|木棺墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin Graves|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弥生時代の墓制|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487466}}
* {{ill|Jar burial|en|Jar burial|WD=Q4244233|lt=Jar burial|1=Jar burial}}
* {{ill|Stone Coffin Graves|ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|1=Sekkan (Sarcophagus)|lt=Sarcophagus|qq=墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Coffin Graves|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekkan|qq=}}
* {{ill|Burial Practices of the Yayoi Period|ja|弥生時代の墓制|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Burial Practices of the Yayoi Period|13=User:Immanuelle/Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|qq=}}
